title: SPERMIDINE
description: Spermidine is a naturally occurring polyamine compound found in various foods and produced by the body that acts as a potent autophagy inducer. It works by stimulating cellular cleanup processes, protecting against age-related decline, and supporting cardiovascular, cognitive, and metabolic health. Research suggests it may extend lifespan and reduce mortality risk through its effects on cellular maintenance and anti-inflammatory mechanisms.

research.title: Spermidine in health and disease
research.summary: Comprehensive review identifying spermidine as a caloric restriction mimetic that delays aging and protects from age-associated diseases by inducing autophagy and cellular renewal processes.

research.title: Effects of Spermidine Supplementation on Cognition and Biomarkers in Older Adults With Subjective Cognitive Decline: A Randomized Clinical Trial
research.summary: Clinical trial in older adults showing spermidine supplementation improved cognitive performance and various biomarkers in participants with subjective cognitive decline.

research.title: Dietary spermidine improves cognitive function
research.summary: Study demonstrating that dietary spermidine supplementation enhances cognitive function through mechanisms involving autophagy and neuroprotection in animal models and human observations.

research.title: Spermidine delays aging in humans
research.summary: Epidemiological evidence showing higher dietary spermidine intake correlates with reduced mortality risk equivalent to being 5.7 years younger in biological age.

research.title: Spermidine is essential for fasting-mediated autophagy and longevity
research.summary: Research demonstrating spermidine's critical role in mediating the autophagy-inducing and lifespan-extending effects of fasting and caloric restriction.

research.title: Spermidine reduces neuroinflammation and soluble amyloid beta in an Alzheimer's disease mouse model
research.summary: Animal study showing spermidine reduced neuroinflammation and amyloid-beta accumulation, suggesting potential protective effects against Alzheimer's disease pathology.

research.title: Safety and tolerability of spermidine supplementation in mice and older adults with subjective cognitive decline
research.summary: Safety study confirming spermidine supplementation is well-tolerated in older adults with minimal adverse effects, supporting its viability for long-term use.

research.title: Protective Role of Spermidine in Colitis and Colon Carcinogenesis
research.summary: Research demonstrating spermidine's protective effects against inflammatory bowel disease and colon cancer development through anti-inflammatory mechanisms.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report positive experiences with spermidine supplementation, particularly for longevity and cognitive benefits, though effects are subtle and take time. Most users take it as part of a comprehensive longevity stack rather than for immediate noticeable effects.

effects.label: Longevity Marker:
effects.description: Studies show strongest correlation with reduced mortality among 146 nutrients tested, with effects comparable to being 5.7 years younger

effects.label: Dietary vs Supplemental:
effects.description: Research emphasizes dietary sources (mushrooms, beans, wheat germ) may be more effective than high-dose supplementation

effects.label: Gradual Impact:
effects.description: Users typically don't notice dramatic short-term effects, with benefits appearing over weeks to months of consistent use

effects.label: Synergistic Effects:
effects.description: Often combined with other longevity supplements (NMN, resveratrol) as part of comprehensive anti-aging protocols

dosage.label: Typical Supplemental Dose:
dosage.description: 1-10mg daily from supplements, with most commercial products containing 1-10mg per serving

dosage.label: David Sinclair Protocol:
dosage.description: Referenced taking 1g daily, though this appears to be a misstatement as studies use 1-2mg ranges

dosage.label: Dietary Sources:
dosage.description: 2 tablespoons wheat germ provides approximately 6-12mg, making food sources highly cost-effective

dosage.label: Clinical Study Doses:
dosage.description: Human trials typically use 1.2mg daily with positive results on cognition and biomarkers

sideEffects.label: Increased Body Heat:
sideEffects.description: Common reported side effect requiring some users to microdose to avoid intense warming sensation

sideEffects.label: Exercise Odor:
sideEffects.description: Some users report unusual body odor during intense exercise on days they take spermidine

sideEffects.label: Sleep Disturbance:
sideEffects.description: Higher doses may cause sleep issues in sensitive individuals, leading users to take lower amounts

sideEffects.label: Minimal Serious Effects:
sideEffects.description: Generally well-tolerated with safety studies confirming low risk profile in long-term use

availability.label: Multiple Brands:
availability.description: Available from DoNotAge (8-16mg), Primeadine (lower dose with wheat germ), DoubleWood (10mg), and other supplement companies

availability.label: Cost Considerations:
availability.description: Relatively expensive compared to obtaining spermidine from dietary sources like wheat germ, mushrooms, or beans

availability.label: Food Sources Preferred:
availability.description: Many users and researchers recommend getting spermidine from whole food sources rather than isolated supplements for better efficacy
